title: Validate split skill source model across packaging, execution-root, onboarding, and upgrade flows
description: Update and run the focused test suite covering packaged asset helpers, Codex skill source lookup, onboarding installs/scaffolds, and upgrade manifest behavior for the new split between `templates/skills/` and repo-root `.agents/skills/` exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover bundled asset helper expectations for `templates/skills/` and operator-only exceptions in `.agents/skills/`.
Tests verify `prepare_isolated_execution_root()` still places allowlisted Codex skills under the execution root skills directory while sourcing from the new split roots.
Targeted onboarding and upgrade tests pass with expectations updated from `.agents/skills/**` to `templates/skills/**` where appropriate.

handoff_summary.completed: Ran 213 tests across: test_assets.py (42), test_onboarding_assets.py (20), test_onboarding_upgrade.py (18), test_onboarding_scaffold.py (24), test_config_wiring_phase3.py (55 + 5 subtests), test_cli_upgrade.py (27). All passed. Coverage confirms: (1) TestSkillsFallbackToBundled suite passes including two new tests for role/investigator; (2) TestMemorySkillCopiedToExecRoot passes - memory resolves from bundled agents_skills in isolated exec root; (3) prepare_isolated_execution_root correctly sources subagent skills from bundled templates/skills; (4) install_skill_templates exported from agent_takt.onboarding namespace; (5) templates/skills/ files recorded as user_owned=false in manifest; (6) evaluate_upgrade_actions handles templates/skills/ entries correctly (new/user_added); (7) regression confirmed: templates/agents/ still user_owned=true.
handoff_summary.risks: Known limitation from dependency bead B-53e0b542: upgrade.py _BUNDLED_ASSET_PREFIXES and _compute_bundled_catalog do not yet include templates/skills/, so takt upgrade will not track or update these files at runtime. Tests mock _compute_bundled_catalog to work around this — actual upgrade behavior for templates/skills/ files requires a follow-up bead.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: No new design decisions made. Tests validate the split skill source model as implemented by dependency beads.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: 213 tests pass across all targeted modules. TestSkillsFallbackToBundled (6 tests): all pass including test_role_investigator_bundled_skill_exists, test_role_investigator_fallback_to_bundled_when_absent_in_project, test_memory_skill_falls_back_to_bundled_agents_skills, and test_templates_skills_dir_takes_priority_over_agents_skills. TestMemorySkillCopiedToExecRoot (4 tests): memory resolves correctly in isolated exec root for both codex backend developer and tester agents. TestWriteAssetsManifest: templates/skills/ recorded as user_owned=false with correct SHA. TestEvaluateUpgradeActions: templates/skills/ new/user_added actions work via mocked catalog. Regression: templates/agents/ still user_owned=true confirmed.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: upgrade.py _compute_bundled_catalog does not yet enumerate templates/skills/ — tests work around this by mocking. Runtime takt upgrade will not track templates/skills/ files without a follow-up bead adding templates/skills/ to _BUNDLED_ASSET_PREFIXES.

execution_history.summary: Lease expired and bead was requeued
Used legacy remaining-text fallback for tester bead because verdict was omitted.
Worker execution failed: Agent timed out after 1500 seconds
Requeued blocked bead after transient infrastructure/auth error

All 213 targeted tests pass across the full split-skill validation suite. TestSkillsFallbackToBundled passes in full (6 tests including both new ones for role/investigator and templates_skills priority). TestMemorySkillCopiedToExecRoot (4 tests) passes with memory resolving correctly in isolated exec roots for both developer and tester agent types. All onboarding, scaffold, upgrade, and CLI upgrade tests pass with templates/skills/ coverage confirmed.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `.agents/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**
